meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: Solve Deployment Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship production-ready apps faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Deployment failure is a workflow bottleneck, not a coding error. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you standardize your infrastructure-as-code, eliminating environment drift. When paired with Claude Code, you automate the complex orchestration of CI/CD pipelines, allowing you to ship robust, client-ready applications in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on a client deadline, the local environment is pristine, but the cloud deployment is a graveyard of cryptic error codes and dependency hell. You aren't just missing a deployment; you are losing billable hours and credibility. Every minute spent debugging a CI/CD pipeline is a minute stolen from your actual craft.

It is time to stop reinventing the wheel. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you move from manual, fragile deployments to a hardened, repeatable architecture that ships on autopilot. This is the difference between a stressed freelancer grinding for fixes and a high-level architect delivering production-ready value with surgical precision.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root project structure. This ensures all future deployments follow a standardized, production-grade configuration that minimizes runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity production-ready template for a full-stack SaaS application, including environment variable management and Docker support.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC)
steps.description: Define your cloud resources programmatically. This step prevents 'it works on my machine' syndrome by codifying the environment requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Terraform/Pulumi configuration files within the Antigravity template to provision a scalable cloud environment optimized for low-latency web hosting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate infrastructure definitions for AWS and configure project constants'

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipeline Injection
steps.description: Inject a robust deployment pipeline that triggers on merge. This removes manual intervention from the delivery process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that integrates with the Antigravity template to automate testing, building, and staging deployment on every push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a multi-stage CI/CD pipeline inside the .github/workflows directory'

steps.title: Sanitize Secrets and Environment Variables
steps.description: Ensure sensitive data is handled securely. Never hardcode credentials; use the Antigravity template's built-in vault integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the project to use secure environment variable injection, ensuring no secrets are committed to the repository.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement environment variable sanitization and vault integration'

steps.title: Execute Dry-Run Deployment
steps.description: Validate the entire stack before going live. This catches configuration errors without impacting production uptime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a simulation of the deployment process using the current Antigravity configuration to identify potential bottleneck or permission issues.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute a dry-run deployment and output any potential configuration conflicts'

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' branch that mirrors production to catch environment-specific bugs before your client sees them.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of your project-specific naming conventions across multiple sessions.
Keep your Dockerfiles lean; use multi-stage builds in your Antigravity template to slash deployment times by 60%.
Monitor infrastructure costs by adding a 'cost-estimate' step in your GitHub Actions workflow for every pull request.
Version your environment variables in a separate encrypted store so you can roll back infrastructure without redeploying code.
content.next_steps: Once your deployment is stable, implement automated E2E testing to ensure every future feature update is as bulletproof as your initial deployment.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your infrastructure and start building the features your clients actually pay for. By standardizing your workflow, you unlock the ability to scale your freelance business effortlessly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
